mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS over recent months lowers the discount rate on equities and eases financial conditions, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is falling from an elevated level.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS has declined for 2 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not outperform the unconditional 20-day return baseline, or if the average 20-day forward return is negative despite falling FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation rate above the policy rate implies tighter real financial conditions and higher rate-cut uncertainty, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation running above FEDFUNDS on a 3-month annualized basis
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-to-FEDFUNDS spread is positive in triggered states but 60-day SPX returns are not lower than baseline, or if the sign of the average return is positive across most triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and weaker earnings expectations, so the S&P 500 should underperform over the next 20 trading days after a clear labor-market deterioration.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not materially worse after a 0.3 pp three-month rise in UNRATE, or if the hit rate is not below 50% in triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A broad rise in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumer spending and revenue growth, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings revisions turn positive.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of rising RSAFS fail to produce positive excess 20-day SPX returns versus the sample baseline, or if the rolling rank correlation stays near zero for 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO after a prior dip reflects improving industrial supply conditions and firmer cyclical activity, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through a short-horizon growth surprise effect.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises month over month after a negative prior reading
failure_condition: Reject if positive monthly reversals in INDPRO are followed by non-positive 5-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the signal disappears in rolling 1-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST and RSAFS both improve, housing activity and retail demand confirm a synchronized demand upswing, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through stronger earnings breadth.
candidate_trigger: both HOUST and RSAFS are above their 6-month moving averages
failure_condition: Reject if joint strength in HOUST and RSAFS does not produce higher 60-day SPX returns than either series alone, or if the combined signal has no incremental lift over the base rate.

mechanism_narrative: In a cooling labor-market regime, a rise in UNRATE combined with slowing PAYEMS growth marks an earnings-recession warning, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises while PAYEMS growth is negative over the same 3-month window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint deterioration in UNRATE and PAYEMS does not precede negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal is weaker than either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: After a policy-rate peak, stable or falling CPIAUCSL with still-high FEDFUNDS implies real-rate relief ahead, so S&P 500 returns should turn positive over the next 60 trading days as tightening risk fades.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains above 4% while CPIAUCSL inflation is flat or falling for 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if flat or falling CPIAUCSL under high FEDFUNDS does not precede above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if returns are negative in most triggered instances.
